title: Zowe MCP Evals Question Set
description: Schema for eval question set YAML files. Ansible-style assertions: each assertion is an object with a key (toolCall, toolCallOrder, answerContains) and an optional name.

config.description: Set-level eval configuration: backend (mock or native), repetitions, minimum success rate, and optional system prompt overrides.

name.description: Short identifier for this question set (e.g. datasets, pagination, jobs). Used when running evals with --set <name>.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this set tests (e.g. 'List data sets and basic context').

repetitions.description: Number of times each question is run per eval. Results are aggregated to compute pass rate.

minSuccessRate.description: Minimum fraction of runs that must pass (0–1) for the set to be considered successful.

mock.description: Use the mock backend. A temporary mock data directory is created via init-mock before starting the server.

initArgs.description: Arguments passed to init-mock (after --output <dir>). Typical: '--preset default' or '--preset pagination'.

native.description: Use the native (SSH) backend. Requires a config file and credentials.

mockServers.description: Generic mock servers started before the MCP server. Each entry can optionally run an init command in a harness-managed temp directory so data and config are created fresh per eval run.

items.description: A single generic mock server definition.

name.description: Human-readable name used in log messages (e.g. 'endevor-ews').

cliScript.description: Absolute path to the Node.js CLI script (invoked as 'node <cliScript>').

initArgs.description: Arguments for the init subcommand run once in a harness-managed temp directory. Use {dataDir} as a placeholder for the temp data sub-path. Example: 'init ENDEVOR --output {dataDir}'

configTemplate.description: Optional config template written to a temp JSON file and passed as --config. String values may contain {dataDir} and {port} placeholders.

configOutputName.description: Filename of the config file created by initArgs (default: 'mock-ews-config.json').

configFlag.description: CLI flag for passing the config file to the server start command (default: '--config').

serveArgs.description: Full argument string for starting the server (after 'node <cliScript>'). Replaces the default 'serve --port <port>' construction. Use ${availablePort} for dynamic port allocation (first free port from 10000). {dataDir} and {port} are also substituted. Config file (from initArgs or configTemplate) is auto-injected after the first word. Example: 'serve --port ${availablePort}'

startArgs.description: Extra arguments appended to the start command. {dataDir} and {port} are substituted. Ignored when serveArgs is set.

port.description: Fixed port for the server. Ignored when serveArgs contains ${availablePort} (dynamic allocation). Defaults to 8080 when neither serveArgs nor port are set.

pluginName.description: When set, the harness auto-creates a CLI bridge connection for this plugin pointing to localhost:<port>. Plugin defaults are applied (e.g. for 'endevor': user/password/basePath/etc.), so no separate cliPluginConfiguration entry is needed. Explicit cliPluginConfiguration take precedence.

cliPluginConfiguration.description: Connection config per CLI plugin name. The harness writes each entry as a temp JSON file and passes --cli-plugin-configuration <name>=<file> to the server. For the 'endevor' plugin, host/user/password/protocol/basePath/pluginParams are pre-filled; only 'port' is usually needed.

additionalProperties.description: Connection config for one CLI plugin. All fields optional; plugin-specific defaults are applied by the harness.

port.description: Port number. Accepts an integer or an env-var placeholder string (e.g. "${DB2_PORT}") which is resolved at load time.

database.description: Database name / Db2 DDF location name (e.g. MYDBLOC). Passed as-is to the connection profile.

pluginParams.description: Plugin-specific params passed to the connection profile. All entries are added in addition to the standard fields (use for fields not in the standard interface, e.g. subsystem, region). Default for endevor: { instance: 'ENDEVOR' }.

cliPluginsDir.description: Override for the CLI plugins directory (--cli-plugins-dir). Defaults to <server-dist>/tools/cli-bridge/plugins/.

cliPluginDescVariant.description: Description variant for CLI bridge tools (--cli-plugin-desc-variant). Values: cli | optimized or any custom name.

mcpServerScript.description: Path to an alternative MCP server script. When set, starts this instead of zowe-mcp-server (e.g. for code4z-gen-ai cross-comparison evals).

mcpServerArgs.description: Extra args for the alternative MCP server, one string split on whitespace.

toolAliases.description: Maps actual tool names from the server to canonical assertion names in the question YAML. Applied after each run.

systemPrompt.description: Override the default system prompt entirely.

systemPromptAddition.description: Appended to the default system prompt.

skip.description: When set, the entire question set is skipped with this reason shown in the log.

questionsFrom.description: Load questions from another named question set (e.g. 'endevor'). When set, a local 'questions' array is not required.

questions.description: List of questions. Each question has an id, a user prompt, and assertions.

items.description: A single eval question: the prompt sent to the agent and the assertions checked against tool calls and final answer.

id.description: Unique identifier for this question within the set. Use kebab-case (e.g. list-members-src-cobol).

prompt.description: The user prompt sent to the AI agent.

preset.description: Override mock preset for this question when using mock backend.

skip.description: When set, this question is skipped with this reason shown in the log.

assertions.description: Assertions block. An array (all must pass), or an object with allOf/anyOf.

AssertionItem.description: One assertion item: leaf assertion (toolCall, toolCallOrder, answerContains) or composite (allOf/anyOf). Exactly one assertion key must be present.

name.description: Optional human-readable name for this assertion. Shown in failure messages.

toolCall.description: Unified tool-call assertion. Specify tool (single), tools (any of), or oneOf (per-tool args). Optional count/minCount.

tools.description: Any of these tools matches (no per-tool args).

oneOf.description: Any of these tool specs matches (with per-tool args).

args.description: Argument matchers (partial match). String values: case-insensitive substring. Values may be { pattern, flags? } for RegExp.test on string actuals (default flags 'i'). Special key 'validDsn': canonical DSN string for server-equivalent matching.

count.description: Exact number of total tool calls expected.

toolCallOrder.description: Ordered tool-call sequence. Each step: tool (single) or tools (any of), plus optional args.

items.description: One step: use tool (single) or tools (any of), plus optional args.

tool.description: Single tool name for this step.

tools.description: Step matches when any of these tools is called.

args.description: Argument matchers (partial match). String values: case-insensitive substring; values may be { pattern, flags? } for regex on string actuals (default flags 'i'). If array, step matches when actual args match any element. Special key 'validDsn' for canonical DSN matching.

answerContains.description: Assert final answer contains a substring or matches a regex pattern.

substring.description: Literal substring that must appear in the final answer (case-sensitive).

pattern.description: Regex pattern that the final answer must match.

allOf.description: All nested assertions must pass (logical AND).

anyOf.description: At least one nested assertion must pass (logical OR).
